title: vicia sativa subsp. uncinata
intro: Vicia sativa subsp. uncinata, also known as spring vetch or narrow-leaved vetch, is a subspecies of the annual legume Vicia sativa. It is a climbing or twining perennial plant with medicinal properties attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and flavonoids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Vicia sativa subsp. uncinata is typically prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
